reasoning:
The base rate (average 30-day count) is 542 protests. For the first resolution date (Feb 22, 2026), the relevant 30-day window is Jan 23 to Feb 22. During this period, there was a massive nationwide strike on February 8, 2026 to mark the second anniversary of the 2024 elections, with 'shutter-down' strikes in 'hundreds of places' and torchlight marches in all major cities involving 'hundreds of thousands' of people. ACLED counts each location's distinct protest as a separate event. Because of the scale of this mobilization (also called the 'start of a long movement' by TTAP leaders), plus the Jan 11 PTI rally and general ongoing unrest over Imran Khan's imprisonment, the count for late Jan to mid Feb is highly likely to exceed the average of 18 protests per day. I predict 85% for Feb 22.

For the second resolution date (Mar 17), the 30-day window is Feb 15 to Mar 17. Ramadan is forecasted to begin in Pakistan around February 18-19, 2026. Historically, protest activity in Islamic countries experiences a significant suppression during the month of Ramadan due to daytime fasting and altered social schedules. Since the entire Mar 17 window falls during Ramadan, the massive event counts from early February will rotate out of the 30-day rolling sum, and new protests will likely be much slower. Thus, the risk of the 30-day window dropping below the high 542 threshold increases substantially. I place the probability for Mar 17 at 45%.
